success_surface: Pattern-based cell formatting with whole-word regex matching using openpyxl for style preservation
effective_behaviors: Probe worksheet structure (max row/column, headers) before implementing changes
Handle openpyxl font/color attribute variations defensively with try-except or type checking
Use regex word boundaries (\b) for whole-word matching to avoid partial substring matches
Extract patterns from sample/reference data rather than hardcoding values
Explicitly validate and skip empty/null cells before processing to prevent errors
Track operation statistics (modified count, ignored count, unique items) for validation
evidence: Agent first executed inspection code to verify sheet names, max columns (BD=56), and max rows (308)
Encountered AttributeError when accessing cell.font.color.rgb directly; corrected by checking hasattr and using str().upper() conversion
Used re.escape(pattern) + r'\b...\b' for safe whole-word regex matching avoiding false positives like 'too' in 'took'
Collected patterns from 254 pre-formatted cells instead of assuming fixed values (419 unique patterns extracted)
Added explicit condition: if not cell.value or not str(cell.value).strip(): continue
Printed counters: cells_modified, cells_ignored, total unique patterns for verification

memory_items.title: Explore Before Execute
memory_items.lesson: Verify workbook structure (sheets, dimensions, headers) before applying transformations
memory_items.generalization: Always load and inspect target ranges, max rows/columns, and header schema prior to modification to avoid off-by-one errors or wrong sheet assumptions

memory_items.title: Defensive Style Attribute Access
memory_items.lesson: openpyxl font/color objects have varying attributes across versions; use hasattr checks
memory_items.generalization: When reading cell styles (font.bold, font.color.rgb/indexed), wrap in try-except or check attribute existence to handle null RGB objects or version differences

memory_items.title: Whole Word Pattern Matching
memory_items.lesson: Use regex word boundaries (\b) to match complete words only
memory_items.generalization: For search-and-format tasks, construct regex patterns with \b anchors to prevent partial matches within longer strings

memory_items.title: Dynamic Pattern Inference
memory_items.lesson: Extract search patterns from reference/formatted cells rather than hardcoding
memory_items.generalization: When task involves conditional formatting based on content patterns, scan exemplar cells first to build dynamic pattern lists

memory_items.title: Track Operation Metrics
memory_items.lesson: Count processed, modified, and skipped items for validation
memory_items.generalization: Maintain running counters during batch operations to enable sanity checks and debugging of transformation scope

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Defensive Cell Style Reading
When accessing `cell.font` attributes in openpyxl, some color properties may be null or version-specific:
- Use `hasattr()` checks before accessing `.rgb` or `.indexed`
- Convert color values via `str().upper()` before comparison
- Wrap style checks in try-except blocks for resilience

### Whole Word Search Patterns
For text pattern matching that requires whole-word semantics:
- Construct regex with `\b` word boundary markers: `r'\b' + re.escape(pattern) + r'\b'`
- This prevents false matches where substrings exist inside longer words
- Always escape special characters in dynamic patterns with `re.escape()`

### Dynamic Pattern Extraction
When search criteria are not explicitly provided:
- Scan reference/formatted cells to extract candidate patterns programmatically
- Build unique sets from discovered content rather than hardcoding expected values
- Track extracted pattern counts for validation purposes

### Pre-Execution Workbook Inspection
Before modifying spreadsheets:
- Load workbook in inspection mode first to confirm sheet names, dimensions, and target column/row indices
- Verify headers and data types match expectations
- Document max_row, max_column, and relevant column letters early for reference

### Operation Metrics Tracking
During batch cell operations:
- Maintain counters for modified_cells, skipped_empty, unique_patterns, etc.
- Log final statistics to confirm expected scope of changes applied
